CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, name VARCHAR(50), created_at TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, created_at) VALUES (1, 'Test Name', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
SELECT internal_type(id) FROM test_table WHERE id = 1;
SELECT internal_type(name) FROM test_table WHERE name = 'Test Name';
SELECT internal_type(created_at) FROM test_table WHERE created_at > '2023-01-01';
CREATE TABLE another_table (description TEXT, age SMALLINT);
INSERT INTO another_table (description, age) VALUES ('Sample Description', 25);
SELECT internal_type(description) FROM another_table WHERE age = 25;
SELECT internal_type(age) FROM another_table WHERE description LIKE 'Sample%';
SELECT t.id, internal_type(t.name) FROM test_table t JOIN another_table a ON t.id = a.age;
SELECT t.id, internal_type(a.description) FROM test_table t LEFT JOIN another_table a ON t.id = a.age WHERE a.age IS NOT NULL;
SELECT internal_type((SELECT age FROM another_table WHERE description = 'Sample Description')) AS age_type;
SELECT internal_type(internal_type(name)) FROM test_table WHERE id = 1;
SELECT internal_type(name) AS name_type, internal_type(created_at) AS created_at_type FROM test_table WHERE name IS NOT NULL;
SELECT internal_type(CAST('2023-01-01' AS TIMESTAMP)) AS timestamp_type;
